title: Warm Indian Peanuts Rasam
description: A grassy, velvety brunch‑mood rasam packed with roasted peanuts and smoky heat, dairy‑free and served warm from a wok.
instructions: Place peanuts, tamarind pulp, tomatoes, coriander, mustard seeds, cumin seeds, dried red chili, asafoetida, black pepper, salt, lemon juice, and water in a wok and bring to a gentle boil over medium heat.
Once boiling, reduce heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking and ensure even cooking.
After simmering, mash the tomatoes and peanuts slightly with a spoon for a smoother texture.
Increase heat to high and bring the rasam to a rapid boil, letting the tempering release aromas.
Reduce heat to medium and let the rasam bubble for an additional 5 minutes, adjusting salt if needed.
Stir in a splash of lemon juice for brightness and remove from heat.
Ladle the warm rasam into bowls, garnish with fresh coriander, and serve immediately.
